food_subgroup: Blended yogurt and mango drink, sweetened

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no specific concept for Mango Lassi. Nearest parent is a yogurt-based fruit beverage. A new concept for South Asian lassi beverages is recommended.

main_ingredients: ripe mango pulp (Alphonso or Kesar mango)

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of mango lassi is 1 glass (approximately 300ml / 310g) — a thick, creamy, bright-orange-yellow blended drink of yogurt and mango pulp, sweet with a pleasant yogurt tang.

nutrient_source.title: Mango Lassi — ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods, Entry EX000036
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research — National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Exact match in ICMR-NIN under entry EX000036 — Mango Lassi, standard preparation (yogurt, mango pulp, sugar).

gi_source: University of Sydney GI Database — estimated from yogurt and mango pulp combination; yogurt protein and fat moderate the GI of the mango sugars.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium approximately 130mg per glass (310g) — very low. High potassium (428mg per glass) from mango and yogurt is beneficial for blood pressure.
hypertension.guidance: Suitable for hypertensive patients — very low sodium, high potassium from mango. The calcium from yogurt is also beneficial for blood pressure regulation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate GI (~52); glycaemic load per glass (310g) is approximately 25. The yogurt moderates the GI of mango, but the total sugar content per glass (~44g) is high.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Diabetic patients should limit to half a glass (150ml) and avoid added sugar. Request sugar-free mango lassi or use less sweet mango. The yogurt protein moderates the glycaemic response significantly.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Mango is one of the richest fruit sources of vitamin C (15.5mg/100g) and beta-carotene/vitamin A (88mcg RAE/100g); yogurt provides probiotics and calcium.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Suitable for patients with rheumatoid arthritis — the vitamin C and beta-carotene from mango support collagen synthesis and have anti-inflammatory antioxidant effects. The probiotics from yogurt support gut-immune axis health.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mango Lassi is India's most popular yogurt beverage — a smooth, thick, intensely fruity drink made by blending whole-milk yogurt with ripe mango pulp (traditionally Alphonso or Kesar mango), sugar, and sometimes a pinch of cardamom. Chilled and served immediately. The mango provides a bright, tropical sweetness and the yogurt adds a creamy body and mild tang. Rich in vitamin C, beta-carotene, and calcium. Available at virtually every Indian restaurant in Singapore as a standard beverages menu item. The ratio of mango to yogurt affects both texture and sweetness.
common_user_queries: how much sugar in mango lassi

confidence_reason: Exact match found in ICMR-NIN under entry EX000036 — Mango Lassi, standard preparation.
notes: Contains dairy (yogurt). Not vegan or dairy-free. Cholesterol approximately 25mg per glass (310g). Vitamin A is notably high from mango beta-carotene (88mcg RAE/100g, approximately 273mcg RAE per glass). The sugar content varies significantly based on mango sweetness and added sugar — values assume standard restaurant preparation with added sugar.
